headline: FIRST-BASELINE assessment for South Korea (rotation queue, never previously assessed). Published composite 62.5 (Established band, rank 35). Research finds significant POSITIVE structural evidence not captured in baseline: (1) Constitutional Court April 2025 unanimous removal of President Yoon for martial law decree; (2) Feb 2026 Yoon convicted of insurrection, life sentence — first president in modern Korean history convicted; (3) Lee Jae-myung administration launched government-wide probe of officials who cooperated in martial law execution; (4) Lee proposed constitutional amendment for two four-year terms with midterm elections (accountability strengthening); (5) Dec 2025 democracy resilience anniversary marked institutionally. Mitigating signals: Lee's own legal exposure required Constitutional Court intervention; structural reforms still in proposal phase. Net assessment: composite of 65.0 vs published 62.5 — sub-threshold delta of +2.5 pts; band remains Established but with positive trajectory.
key_evidence: April 4, 2025: Constitutional Court unanimously upheld Yoon's impeachment for martial law decree, holding he 'violated' basic rights — restoration of democratic order.

Feb 19, 2026: Yoon convicted of insurrection and sentenced to life imprisonment; defense minister 30 years; four other senior officials 3-18 years — unprecedented criminal accountability for executive abuse.
Lee Jae-myung won June 3, 2025 election with ~80% turnout (highest since 1997) on platform of democratic restoration.
Lee administration launched government-wide probe of officials who cooperated in martial law execution.
Lee proposed September 2025 constitutional amendment changing presidential term to two four-year terms with midterm accountability mechanism.
Dec 2025: One-year-anniversary celebrations marked democracy resilience institutionally.
Mitigating: Korean institutions required Constitutional Court intervention to remove Yoon; political polarization remains high; Lee's own pre-presidency legal cases sustained.

evidence.finding: Yoon Suk Yeol punished for trying to unravel democracy; first South Korean president criminally convicted of insurrection.
Constitutional Court unanimously upheld impeachment, finding Yoon 'violated basic rights' — institutional accountability functioning.
Lee government launched probe of martial law collaborators; constitutional amendment proposal for 2-term presidency with midterm accountability.
One year after martial law crisis, South Korea celebrates democracy's resilience — institutional pattern of bearing real cost to maintain commitments.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3.6 (+0.1). National-Assembly-led detection of executive overreach occurred within hours of martial law decree; National Human Rights Commission active.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 3.5 (unchanged). No specific in-window evidence of empathy-dimension change.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3.6 (+0.1). Rapid Constitutional Court action (Apr 2025), criminal trials concluded (Feb 2026), Lee administration probe launched — responsiveness elevated.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 3.1 (+0.1). LGBT recognition still incomplete and conscientious objection issues remain; modest positive movement under Lee.
scoring_rationale.BND: 4.0 (unchanged). Strong institutional boundaries demonstrated by rejection of martial law overreach; preservation of civilian government.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 3.9 (+0.4). AB1/AB2/AB3 all elevated by criminal accountability for highest official; AB4 Systemic Learning evidenced by reform proposals; AB5 Reparative Action via removal and prosecution.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.6 (+0.1). S2 Long-Term Impact strengthened by constitutional amendment proposal; S4 Structural Critique demonstrated by acknowledgment of executive-power structural weakness.
scoring_rationale.INT: 3.7 (+0.2). I1 Consistency Under Pressure: Korean institutions bore significant cost (months of crisis) to maintain democratic norms; I5 Resilience of Care: practices survived leadership stress test.

negativeSignals: Political polarization sustained; Lee's pre-presidency legal cases; conscientious objection treatment; LGBT recognition incomplete.
positiveSignals: Unprecedented criminal accountability for executive abuse; Constitutional Court enforcement of basic rights; high voter turnout (80%); structural reform proposals; democracy-resilience institutional pattern.
sector_context: Established-band democracy demonstrating extraordinary institutional resilience — comparable to Brazil's Bolsonaro accountability process. Provides cross-jurisdictional evidence that Critical-band democratic backsliding can be reversed.
open_actions_resolved: Yoon impeachment/conviction captured as ACC/INT evidence
open_actions_carried: Re-queue South Korea after Lee constitutional amendment vote
Monitor Lee administration's probe of martial-law collaborators outcomes
